description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1), operating in autonomous mode and traveling northeast on Bryant Street, was preparing to make a right on red 
traffic light on 10th Street in San Francisco. After fully stopping at the stop line, it advanced into the crosswalk before yielding to cross 
traffic with the right of way. A bicyclist, riding northwest on the sidewalk along 10th Street, then approached the crosswalk. As the bicyclist 
entered the crosswalk, the operator of the Zoox vehicle disengaged from autonomy and began to manually drive the vehicle forward. The 
bicyclist veered right towards the front of the Zoox vehicle and made contact with the front passenger side door of the Zoox vehicle. The 
bicycle then fell to the ground next to the Zoox vehicle. After stopping for a brief conversation with the bicyclist, the Zoox vehicle operator 
proceeded to make the right turn onto 10th Street, inadvertently driving over the front wheel of the fallen bicycle. The operator completed 
the turn and pulled over to check for damages and exchange information. The Zoox vehicle did not sustain any damage; however, the front 
wheel rim of the bicycle was damaged. There were no reported injuries, and the police were not notified.
Print
Clear Form
